sales.description:
Ancients
SCYTHIA. Geto-Dacians. Coson (ca. after 54 BC). AV stater (22mm, 8.47 gm, 12h). NGC MS 5/5 - 4/5. Sarmatia, Olbia. KOΣΩN, Roman consul (L. Junius Brutus) walking left, accompanied by two lictors fore and aft, ΟΛΒ monogram in left field / Eagle standing left on scepter, with spread wings, clutching laurel wreath in right talons; pellet below raised foot. RPC I 1701a.

From The Peh Family Collection, Part IV. Ex Heritage Auctions, Auction 397 (9 January 2006), lot 12016.  www.HA.com/Tex...

sales.description:
Ancients
Anonymous. Ca. 218-216 BC. AV stater (18mm, 6.84 gm, 5h). NGC Choice XF 5/5 - 2/5, Fine Style, scuffs. Rome. Laureate, janiform head of Dioscuri; dotted border / Oath-taking scene of youth kneeling left, head reverted, holding pig, two soldiers standing facing him on either side, each with grounded spear in left hand and touching pig with sword in right hand; ROMA in exergue. Crawford 28/1 (225-212 BC). Sydenham 69.

Ex Classical Numismatic Group, Auction 103 (14 September 2016), lot ...
